text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST — RESTAGING EXAMINATION

EXAMINATION: CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis with intravenous contrast.

CLINICAL INDICATION: 63-year-old woman with known stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma, right kidney, with bilateral pulmonary metastases. Status post right radical cytoreductive nephrectomy. Now approximately 4 months into first-line immunotherapy (ipilimumab/nivolumab). Restaging evaluation.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following administration of 100 mL of Omnipaque 350 intravenous contrast, acquired in the portal venous phase. Coronal and sagittal multiplanar reformats generated and reviewed. Comparison made to prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis performed approximately 4 months ago at the time of initial staging, and to pre-operative imaging.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and airways: The previously noted right lower lobe metastatic nodule has decreased appreciably in size, now measuring approximately 1.0 x 0.8 cm compared to 1.8 x 1.5 cm on prior staging imaging — a meaningful reduction in overall bulk. The previously identified left upper lobe nodule has similarly diminished, now measuring approximately 0.5 cm in short axis compared to 0.9 cm previously. The right upper lobe nodule, which measured 0.7 cm on prior imaging, is again visualized with no definite change in size, currently measuring 0.7 cm. The left lower lobe nodule previously measuring 0.6 cm is again seen and appears unchanged at approximately 0.6 cm. No new pulmonary nodules are identified. No cavitation within any of the nodules. The central airways are patent. No bronchiectasis.

Pleura: No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. No pleural thickening or nodularity.

Mediastinum and hila: The heart size is within normal limits. No pericardial effusion. The thoracic aorta is unremarkable in caliber. No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy above the threshold for size concern. No anterior mediastinal mass.

Chest wall and osseous structures: No aggressive osseous lesions in the visualized thoracic spine or ribs. Mild degenerative changes in the thoracic spine. Postsurgical changes at the right flank consistent with prior nephrectomy approach.

---

ABDOMEN:

Nephrectomy bed: The right nephrectomy bed is well-defined without evidence of discrete soft tissue recurrence or mass. No fluid collection or seroma of concerning morphology. Expected postsurgical architectural distortion is noted.

Liver: The liver demonstrates homogeneous enhancement without focal lesion. No discrete hypoenhancing or hyperenhancing hepatic nodule identified. No biliary ductal dilatation. Portal vein is patent.

Spleen: Normal size and attenuation. No focal splenic lesion.

Pancreas: Unremarkable. No pancreatic ductal dilatation.

Adrenal glands: The left adrenal gland is visualized and appears normal. The right adrenal gland was preserved at surgery and appears unremarkable without discrete nodule or thickening.

Left kidney: The left kidney shows normal size, contour, and enhancement. No hydronephrosis. No stone. No discrete renal mass.

Retroperitoneum and lymph nodes: No enlarged retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymph nodes are identified. Previously resected hilar and paraaortic nodal stations are without recurrent nodal mass.

Bowel: No bowel obstruction or wall thickening of concern. No evidence of perforation or pneumoperitoneum.

Ascites: None.

---

PELVIS:

Bladder: Normal in appearance, no wall thickening or intraluminal lesion.

Uterus and adnexa: Uterus appears age-appropriately atrophic. No adnexal mass identified.

Pelvic lymph nodes: No pelvic lymphadenopathy.

Osseous structures: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesion in the imaged pelvis, sacrum, or proximal femora. Moderate degenerative changes at the bilateral hip joints and sacroiliac joints.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. The dominant right lower lobe pulmonary metastasis has decreased substantially in size (1.8 cm down to approximately 1.0 cm), and the left upper lobe nodule has similarly shrunk (0.9 cm to 0.5 cm), consistent with treatment response in these lesions following immunotherapy.

2. The right upper lobe and left lower lobe pulmonary nodules appear unchanged from prior staging examination.

3. No new pulmonary nodules, no new intrathoracic disease.

4. The right nephrectomy bed is clear of discrete soft tissue recurrence.

5. No hepatic, adrenal, or osseous lesions identified. No new sites of disease in the abdomen or pelvis.

6. No retroperitoneal nodal recurrence at the previously resected nodal stations.

Overall, compared to staging imaging, there has been a favorable reduction in the larger pulmonary lesions with stability of the smaller nodules, and no evidence of new disease. The findings are consistent with mixed but overall favorable disease response in the setting of ongoing immunotherapy.
